claims.claim_text: MLX is a machine learning framework designed around Apple Silicon and unified memory.
claims.span_hint: line 58 | section: The Inference Runtime Is Part of the Architecture
claims.rationale: The MLX README directly describes MLX as an array framework for Apple silicon and explains its unified-memory model.

claims.claim_text: Ollama provides local model management and an HTTP API for generating responses from pulled models.
claims.span_hint: line 74 | section: Desktop Simplicity: Ollama and MLX
claims.rationale: The Ollama API documentation lists endpoints for generation, model creation, listing, pulling, pushing, and running local models.

claims.claim_text: Ollama lets applications use an OpenAI-like local API flow.
claims.span_hint: line 76 | section: Desktop Simplicity: Ollama and MLX
claims.rationale: The official Ollama OpenAI compatibility page directly supports the claim, but docs.ollama.com is not currently on the allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Lower-bit quantization can reduce memory use for local inference while changing numerical precision.
claims.span_hint: line 80 | section: Desktop Simplicity: Ollama and MLX
claims.rationale: The Transformers quantization guide explains 8-bit and 4-bit quantization as memory-saving reduced-precision loading.

claims.claim_text: Apple Silicon shares memory between CPU and GPU, and MLX operations can use shared memory without explicit transfers.
claims.span_hint: line 82 | section: Desktop Simplicity: Ollama and MLX
claims.rationale: The MLX README states that arrays live in shared memory and can be operated on by supported device types without data transfer.

claims.claim_text: Transformers exposes tokenizer/model loading, device placement, generation parameters, and token-level generation details through Python APIs.
claims.span_hint: lines 102-110 | section: Engineering Control: Hugging Face Transformers
claims.rationale: The Transformers quicktour documents loading tokenizers and models with from_pretrained and running model inference through code.

claims.claim_text: Transformers generation APIs support decoding configuration and generation strategies such as deterministic generation.
claims.span_hint: line 104 | section: Engineering Control: Hugging Face Transformers
claims.rationale: The official generation strategies documentation covers decoding methods and generation configuration behavior.

claims.claim_text: PEFT/LoRA workflows load a base model, attach adapters, and evaluate or merge adapter changes programmatically.
claims.span_hint: line 108 | section: Engineering Control: Hugging Face Transformers
claims.rationale: The PEFT documentation directly covers adapter-based model customization, including LoRA-style workflows.

claims.claim_text: LLM serving consumes KV cache memory for active requests, and KV cache pressure grows with active sequences and context.
claims.rationale: The PagedAttention paper frames KV cache memory management as a central bottleneck in LLM serving.

claims.claim_text: PagedAttention stores KV cache in blocks, inspired by OS paging, to reduce fragmentation and improve serving capacity.
claims.rationale: The PagedAttention paper explicitly describes the block-based KV cache design and its analogy to virtual memory paging.

claims.claim_text: vLLM supports continuous batching for serving overlapping LLM requests.
claims.rationale: The vLLM project page lists continuous batching among its serving capabilities.

claims.claim_text: vLLM provides an OpenAI-compatible API server and containerized serving path.
claims.rationale: The vLLM repository describes the project as an inference and serving engine and lists its OpenAI-compatible API server support.

claims.claim_text: Kubernetes schedules GPUs through vendor device plugins and extended resources such as GPU limits.
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes GPU scheduling documentation explains device-plugin prerequisites and requesting GPUs through resource limits.

claims.claim_text: llama.cpp was written by a single developer in pure C/C++ within days of the LLaMA weights leak.
claims.anchor_text: - The `llama.cpp` project, which powers many desktop runtimes, was originally written in pure C/C++ by a single developer in a matter of days following the initial leak of the LLaMA weights.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - The `llama.cpp` project is a C/C++ local LLM inference project that sits behind many discussions of desktop local-model runtimes.
claims.rationale: The sentence mixes a real project with a specific origin anecdote and leak timeline that is not cleanly supported by an allowlisted primary source.

claims.claim_text: Apple's MLX framework mirrors NumPy and PyTorch API design while compiling operations for Apple hardware.
claims.rationale: The MLX README says its Python API closely follows NumPy and its higher-level packages follow PyTorch-style APIs.

claims.claim_text: Transformers caching can silently consume hundreds of gigabytes of disk space when experimenting with many models.
claims.anchor_text: - Hugging Face Transformers downloads model weights and caches them locally; heavily experimenting with different models without clearing this cache can silently consume hundreds of gigabytes of disk space.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - Hugging Face Transformers downloads model weights and caches them locally; experimenting with many large models without clearing this cache can silently consume substantial disk space.
claims.rationale: The local-cache behavior is real, but the precise 'hundreds of gigabytes' quantity is workload-dependent and not directly supported by the cache documentation.

claims.claim_text: The PagedAttention paper reported KV cache memory waste under 4 percent in evaluated serving settings.
claims.rationale: The PagedAttention paper is the primary source for the reported KV cache memory-waste result.

further_reading.title: Ollama API Documentation
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains Ollama's local HTTP API, generation endpoints, streaming behavior, and model-management endpoints.

further_reading.title: MLX README
further_reading.why_relevant: Describes MLX's Apple Silicon focus, NumPy/PyTorch-like APIs, lazy execution, multi-device support, and unified-memory model.

further_reading.title: Transformers Quicktour
further_reading.why_relevant: Shows the code-first tokenizer, model-loading, and inference workflow used for controlled local evaluation.

further_reading.title: Efficient Memory Management for LLM Serving with PagedAttention
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary source for vLLM's PagedAttention design, KV cache memory management, and reported serving-efficiency results.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes GPU Scheduling
further_reading.why_relevant: Covers the Kubernetes GPU scheduling primitives relevant to running vLLM-style serving containers on GPU nodes.

notes: Quiz answers, exercise scenarios, code blocks, diagrams, and repeated pedagogical framing were intentionally omitted unless they introduced a distinct hard claim.
